checks.a_analysis: The output contains text that faithfully represents the content of the source document. While the document title in the metadata ('Map Imagery Photos Tables Screenshots Diagrams Data Charts I E5Cc') and some table headers (e.g., 'Field', 'Value' in element 116) appear to be AI-generated for structural purposes, they do not constitute fabrication of factual content. All other paragraphs and sentences provided in the JSON have a clear basis in the source PDF.

The output does not contain any fabricated contact information. The only URL mentioned in the source (https://it.nc.gov/gicc on page 41) is not present in the provided JSON segments, and no other phone numbers, emails, or URLs are introduced.

The provided JSON output contains a meta-tag ('_omitted': '34 sections omitted for token budget') indicating that large portions of the document were intentionally excluded from the provided audit sample to manage token limits. Since the beginning and end of the document are present, and the truncation is explicitly labeled as a sample limitation rather than a conversion failure, this is not flagged as a defect.
The tables present in the output (e.g., element 84, 288, 367) contain the correct number of data rows as found in the source PDF. The structural issues in element 117 are handled under C6.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There is a significant text omission in element 305, where a paragraph corresponding to page 38 of the source is truncated mid-sentence. It ends with 'which will', omitting the final 13 words: 'be used to allocate grant money to all states for broadband expansion.' This constitutes a loss of over 25% of that specific paragraph's content. Additionally, in element 288 (Table 1), the description for 'Site Address Street Name' is missing the word 'name'.
checks.b_evidence_source: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) released their serviceable location fabric data, which will be used to allocate grant money to all states for broadband expansion. (Page 38); The full name of the site street including the prefix, name, suffix, type and directions (Page 31, Table 1)
checks.b_evidence_output: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) released their serviceable location fabric data, which will (Element 305); The full name of the site street including the prefix, suffix, type and directions (Element 288)

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Element 117 contains a table that is fundamentally broken and misaligned. It attempts to combine data from different dashboards (Page 14 and Page 38). Specifically, the headers (e.g., 'County', 'Program Scope') do not match the data cells below them (e.g., the first data cell contains 'Households Served' instead of a county name, and the county name 'Harnett' is shifted to the 'Program Scope' column). Summary statistics from the dashboard sidebar are also erroneously inserted into the data rows. This makes the extracted information incorrect and misleading.
checks.b_evidence_source: Figure 11 Table: County: Harnett | Program Scope: County | Program: GREAT | Awardee: Brightspeed (Page 14)
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 117 Table: Header: County | Program Scope | Program | Awardee | ... Row 6: Households Served | Harnett | County | GREAT | Brightspeed | ...

checks.a_analysis: I scanned the JSON output for duplicated content and compared it with the source PDF. The introduction text ('The N.C. Geographic Information Coordinating Council was established...') appears in both the Executive Summary (page 5) and the Introduction (page 6) of the source PDF. The JSON correctly reflects this intentional repetition. No other instances of unintended duplication were found.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent and often incorrectly assigned. While 'Contents' is correctly identified as H1 (Element 10), other major section headers like 'Collaboration for Public Access to Geographic Information' (Element 301) and 'Appendix 1' (Element 383) are also assigned H1, despite being sub-sections of the report. Furthermore, major sections like 'Executive Summary' and 'Introduction' are completely missing from the heading hierarchy and are instead rendered as simple paragraphs (based on the formatting of surrounding elements like 172). This breaks the structural navigation of the document for accessibility users.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table of Contents showing 'Collaboration for Public Access to Geographic Information' as a section within the report.
checks.b_evidence_output:
{
          "type": "heading",
          "level": 1,
          "text": "Collaboration for Public Access to Geographic Information",
          "id": null,
          "lang": null,
          "reading_order": 301
        }

checks.a_analysis: I evaluated the tables in the output, particularly the 'Required attributes for Seamless Parcels' (Element 288) and the 'Outreach presentations' (Element 367). Both match the source PDF's structure in terms of column counts and data alignment. The 'Annexation Submittal Form' (Element 295), which is a screenshot of a web form in the PDF, has been effectively converted into a key-value pair table, preserving its structural information.
The reading order follows the logical flow of the PDF document from the title page through the table of contents and into the main body and appendices.

The Table of Contents is partially converted into a list (Element 11) but then transitions into a series of separate paragraphs (Elements 12-17). This fragments the list structure, which should be continuous for a table of contents.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 11 (list) ends prematurely, and subsequent TOC entries (like elements 12 through 17) are rendered as paragraphs.

checks.a_analysis: Several URLs clearly visible in the source PDF (e.g., https://it.nc.gov/gicc) are present in the text of the JSON but do not contain any hyperlink metadata (<a> tags or URI objects). While the text is preserved, the functional aspect of the links is missing.
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "For more information about the council including the latest meeting information and contact information for council members and staff, please visit https://it.nc.gov/gicc ."
